description: Real-world (costumed) natural language problem statements for training. Sources: EHOP-style (Coli Saar), everyday phrasing. problem_name must match catalog.

pairs.query: I am organizing a party and need to assign guests to tables. No two people who dislike each other can sit at the same table. I want to use as few tables as possible.
We need to schedule committee meetings. Some members sit on multiple committees; two committees that share a member cannot meet at the same time. Minimize the number of time slots.

I have a set of items each with a weight and a value, and a bag with a maximum weight. Choose which items to put in the bag so the total weight does not exceed the limit and the total value is as large as possible.
A truck has limited capacity. I have a list of deliveries each with a weight and a profit. Select which deliveries to load so we stay under capacity and maximize profit.
A salesperson must visit several cities exactly once and return to the starting city. The travel cost between each pair of cities is known. Find the route that minimizes total travel cost.
We need to plan a tour that visits all required locations once and returns to the depot. Minimize the total distance traveled.

I have several facilities I can open, each with an opening cost, and customers that must be assigned to one facility. Each assignment has a cost. Decide which facilities to open and how to assign customers to minimize total opening plus assignment cost.

We have candidate warehouse locations with fixed opening costs and shipping costs to each customer. Each customer must be served from one warehouse. Choose which warehouses to open and assign customers to minimize total cost.
Open facilities with limited capacity and assign customers to open facilities. Minimize opening plus assignment cost. Total demand assigned to a facility cannot exceed its capacity.
Warehouses have maximum capacity. Customers have demands. Choose which warehouses to open and how to assign customers so capacities are not exceeded and total opening and shipping cost is minimized.
Given a graph with edge weights, find a minimum-weight set of edges that connects all vertices (a spanning tree).
We need to connect all nodes in a network at minimum total cost using the available links, each with a cost. No cycles.
Find the shortest path from a source node to a destination node in a directed graph with nonnegative edge costs.
In a network with a start and an end node, find the path with the smallest total cost.

We have jobs that consist of operations on machines. Each operation has a processing time. Each machine can do at most one operation at a time. Schedule operations so that each job's operations run in order and we minimize the time when the last job finishes.
Several jobs, each with a fixed sequence of operations on specific machines. Minimize makespan (time when last job completes) while respecting machine capacity and job order.
Select a subset of vertices in a graph so that no two selected vertices are adjacent. Maximize the sum of weights of selected vertices.
Given a family of sets and a weight for each set, choose a collection of disjoint sets (no two share an element) to maximize total weight.
We have a set of elements and a collection of subsets, each with a weight. Select pairwise disjoint subsets to maximize total weight.
Partition the set of items into a minimum number of subsets such that each subset satisfies a capacity constraint (e.g. total weight below a limit).
Pack items of given sizes into the minimum number of bins of fixed capacity.
Assign each of n jobs to one of n workers. Each assignment has a cost. Find the assignment that minimizes total cost.
We have tasks and agents. Each agent can do each task at some cost. Assign each task to exactly one agent to minimize total cost.
Choose p facility locations from candidates. Assign each customer to the nearest open facility. Minimize total assignment cost (e.g. demand-weighted distance).
Select p facilities and assign each customer to one of them. Minimize the sum of distances (or demand-weighted distances) from customers to their assigned facility.
Vehicles with limited capacity must visit customers with known demand. Each customer is visited once. Minimize total distance or number of vehicles.
Route a fleet of vehicles from a depot to serve customers and return. Each vehicle has a capacity; each customer has a demand. Minimize total routing cost.
Cover all elements of a universe using the minimum number of sets from a given family (or minimum cost if sets have costs).
We have a set of elements that must be covered. Each subset has a cost. Choose subsets so every element is in at least one chosen set and total cost is minimized.
Schedule aircraft landings on a runway. Each aircraft has a time window and a target time. There are minimum separation times between landings. Minimize total penalty for landing early or late.
Given a bipartite graph with edge weights, find a matching (set of edges no two sharing a vertex) of maximum total weight.
Find the maximum amount of flow we can send from a source to a sink in a directed graph with edge capacities.
Send as much flow as possible from source to sink in a capacitated network.
In a directed graph with capacities and costs on edges, send a required amount of flow from source to sink at minimum total cost.
Choose a portfolio of assets to maximize expected return subject to a limit on risk (e.g. variance).
Select investments with given expected returns and covariances. Maximize return while keeping risk below a threshold.
We have a set of constraints and a linear objective. Find values for the variables that satisfy all constraints and optimize the objective.
Minimize or maximize a linear function subject to linear equality and inequality constraints.
